issues.title: MCP failure with Spring AI 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT and MCP 0.10.0-SNAPSHOT in VertexAiGemniChatModel
issues.body:
This code is working with MCP 0.9.0.

Client fails in the initialize()
`    var transport = HttpClientSseClientTransport
        .builder(System.getenv("MCP_WEATHER_SERVER"))
        .build();
    var mcpClient = McpClient.sync(transport).build();

    mcpClient.initialize();
    mcpClient.ping();
   ServerCapabilities capabilities = mcpClient.getServerCapabilities();
...
`

**The response is "io.modelcontextprotocol.spec.McpError: Failed to wait for the message endpoint"**

The error:
`>>> Invoking `getTemperatureByCapital` tool with capital: Tokyo
2025-05-09T09:56:29.766-04:00 ERROR 30435 --- [agents] [oundedElastic-3] o.s.ai.chat.model.MessageAggregator      : Aggregation Error

java.lang.RuntimeException: com.fasterxml.jackson.core.JsonParseException: Unrecognized token 'Failed': was expecting (JSON String, Number, Array, Object or token 'null', 'true' or 'false')
 at [Source: REDACTED (`StreamReadFeature.INCLUDE_SOURCE_IN_LOCATION` disabled); line: 1, column: 7]

comments.body: Can you point to the reproducible example that you have please in github to make this easier to triage.

The code is part of a larger application, which can not be run without configuring database and other dependent servers.

I have shared the repo privately with you @markpollack
The latest changes in Spring AI in the snapshot have introduced this issue, as I reverted to my last commit

@markpollack 
problem is due to a conflict in the ./m2 which had multiple versions of MCP SDK and 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT was getting into a conflict.

Cleared ./m2/../io/modelcontextprotocol and that resolves it. 
There was a similar issue on M7 --> M8
